task_completion.description: Create the parent collection 'Constitutional Law Seminar'
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the left pane (collections pane) of Jurism, there should be a folder named 'Constitutional Law Seminar'.

task_completion.description: Create the three required subcollections under the parent collection
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the left pane of Jurism, under the expanded 'Constitutional Law Seminar' folder, there should be three indented folders named 'First Amendment', 'Due Process', and 'Judicial Review'.

task_completion.description: Populate the 'First Amendment' subcollection with the correct items
task_completion.visual_evidence: When the 'First Amendment' folder is selected in the left pane, the middle pane should list 'New York Times Co. v. Sullivan' and 'Tinker v. Des Moines'. Alternatively, look for screenshots showing these specific items being dragged into the 'First Amendment' folder.

task_completion.description: Populate the 'Due Process' subcollection with the correct items
task_completion.visual_evidence: When the 'Due Process' folder is selected in the left pane, the middle pane should list 'Brown v. Board of Education', 'Miranda v. Arizona', 'Gideon v. Wainwright', 'Obergefell v. Hodges', and 'The Due Process Clause...' (by Poe). Alternatively, look for screenshots showing these specific items being dragged into the 'Due Process' folder.

task_completion.description: Populate the 'Judicial Review' subcollection with the correct items
task_completion.visual_evidence: When the 'Judicial Review' folder is selected in the left pane, the middle pane should list 'Marbury v. Madison', 'The Path of the Law' (by Holmes), and 'Constitutional Fact Review' (by Monaghan). Alternatively, look for screenshots showing these specific items being dragged into the 'Judicial Review' folder.

integrity.description: The agent used the Jurism GUI to create collections and move items.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show the agent clicking on Jurism menus, right-clicking to create collections, and dragging/dropping items, rather than executing scripts or terminal commands to modify the database.

integrity.description: The agent moved the pre-loaded items rather than creating new dummy items.
integrity.visual_evidence: The items in the middle pane should appear as rich library items (with creator names, years, etc. in the columns), not newly created empty items that just have the title.

integrity.description: The task was performed in the actual Jurism application.
integrity.visual_evidence: The interface shown in the screenshots must be the Jurism application, identifiable by its layout (left collections pane, middle items pane, right metadata pane) and icons.

privileged_info_for_vlm:
The task requires organizing 10 real legal references into a specific hierarchy in Jurism.
Parent Collection: 'Constitutional Law Seminar'
Subcollections: 'First Amendment', 'Due Process', 'Judicial Review'
Expected Assignments:
- First Amendment: 'New York Times Co. v. Sullivan', 'Tinker v. Des Moines'
- Due Process: 'Brown v. Board of Education', 'Miranda v. Arizona', 'Gideon v. Wainwright', 'Obergefell v. Hodges', 'The Due Process Clause...' (by Poe)
- Judicial Review: 'Marbury v. Madison', 'The Path of the Law' (by Holmes), 'Constitutional Fact Review' (by Monaghan)
